candidates.name: flatbuffers
candidates.reason: Schema-driven codec; SQL surface design requires a real consumer to pin the right shape (per-message generated table? generic JSON pivot? schema-as-SQL-blob?). Defer until ask.

candidates.name: protobuf
candidates.reason: Schema-driven codec; SQL surface design requires a real consumer to pin the right shape (per-message generated table? generic JSON pivot? schema-as-SQL-blob?). Defer until ask.
candidates.notes: Schema interface is the hard part; flatbuffers + thrift have the same shape.

candidates.name: thrift
candidates.reason: Schema-driven codec; SQL surface design requires a real consumer to pin the right shape (per-message generated table? generic JSON pivot? schema-as-SQL-blob?). Defer until ask.
candidates.notes: Schema problem; less common than protobuf.

candidates.name: webauthn
candidates.notes: Heavy spec; needs handler-side surface as well as SQL.

candidates.name: mathml
candidates.reason: mathml-rs 0.1 does not exist on crates.io. MathML parsing via roll-our-own quick-xml is doable but niche  defer until a consumer asks.
candidates.notes: Niche; specialized for scientific document workflows.

candidates.name: proj
candidates.reason: proj 0.27 wraps the PROJ C library. PROJ does not cross-compile cleanly to wasm32-wasip2 via the wasi-sdk clang  defer until a pure-rust reprojection library covers the workflow.
candidates.notes: Heavy C dependency; defer until needed.

candidates.name: rsa-bignum
candidates.description: Standalone RSA-style bignum modexp + key gen (separate from `rsa` crate).
candidates.reason: Defunct: the rsa extension (rsa 0.9 via num-bigint internally) shipped in round 2, covering key generation + sign/verify/encrypt/decrypt. A separate bignum-backed implementation no longer adds capability.
candidates.notes: bignum has modpow; this would add a key-gen surface specifically.

candidates.name: djvu
candidates.reason: No actively-maintained pure-rust DjVu crate. The historical option is to port djvulibre header parsing  out of scope without a consumer.
candidates.proposed_crate: no crate yet  port djvulibre header
candidates.notes: Niche; defer until a consumer asks.

candidates.name: http-signature
candidates.notes: Used by ActivityPub / Mastodon and the SSF working group.

candidates.name: sqlean-vsv
candidates.notes: csv extension exists but vsv has different shape  worth comparing.
